pg147-p1-s2:
If he is
conscientiously following the program of recovery he can go anywhere your business may call him.
pg147-p1:
that you are not suspicious nor are you trying to run his life so he will be shielded from temptation to drink. If he is
conscientiously following the program of recovery he can go anywhere your business may call him.
pg147-p2-s1: In case he does stumble, even once, you will have to decide whether to let him go.
pg147-p2-s2:
If you are sure he doesn't mean
business, there is no doubt you should discharge him.
pg147-p2-s3:
If, on the contrary, you are sure he is doing his utmost, you may
wish to give him another chance.
pg147-p2-s4:
But you should feel under no obligation to keep him on, for your obligation has been
well discharged already.
pg147-p2:
In case he does stumble, even once, you will have to decide whether to let him go. If you are sure he doesn't mean
business, there is no doubt you should discharge him. If, on the contrary, you are sure he is doing his utmost, you may
wish to give him another chance. But you should feel under no obligation to keep him on, for your obligation has been
well discharged already.
pg147-p3-s1: There is another thing you might wish to do.
pg147-p3-s2:
If your organization is a large one, your junior executives might be
provided with this book.
pg147-p3-s3: You might let them know you have no quarrel with the alcoholics of your organization.
pg147-p3-s4:
These
juniors are often in a difficult position.
pg147-p3-s5: Men under them are frequently their friends.
pg147-p3-s6:
So, for one reason or another,
they cover these men, hoping matters will take a turn for the better.
pg147-p3-s7:
They often jeopardize their own positions by
trying to help serious drinkers who should have been fired long ago, or else given an opportunity to get well.
pg147-p3:
There is another thing you might wish to do. If your organization is a large one, your junior executives might be
provided with this book. You might let them know you have no quarrel with the alcoholics of your organization. These
juniors are often in a difficult position. Men under them are frequently their friends. So, for one reason or another,
they cover these men, hoping matters will take a turn for the better. They often jeopardize their own positions by
trying to help serious drinkers who should have been fired long ago, or else given an opportunity to get well.
pg147-p4-s1: After reading this book, a junior executive can go to such a man and say approximately this, "Look here, Ed.
pg147-p4-s2:
Do you want
to stop drinking or not?
pg147-p4-s3: You put me on the spot every time you get drunk.
pg147-p4-s4: It isn't fair to me or the firm.
pg147-p4-s5:
I have been
learning something about alcoholism.
pg147-p4-s6: If you are an alcoholic, you are a mighty sick man.
pg147-p4-s8:
The firm
wants to help you get over it, and if you are interested, there is a way out.
pg147-p4-s9:
If you take it, your past will be
forgotten
pg147-p4:
After reading this book, a junior executive can go to such a man and say approximately this, "Look here, Ed. Do you want
to stop drinking or not? You put me on the spot every time you get drunk. It isn't fair to me or the firm. I have been
learning something about alcoholism. If you are an alcoholic, you are a mighty sick man. You act like one. The firm
wants to help you get over it, and if you are interested, there is a way out. If you take it, your past will be
forgotten
